items.title: Arrive Galeao International Airport
items.detail: Flight arrives at GIG Terminal 2. Take Uber or taxi to Copacabana/Ipanema ~R$80-120 (40 min depending on traffic). Or airport bus BRT Ligeirinho to Zona Sul.

items.title: Check In — Ipanema Hotel
items.detail: Hotel near Ipanema Beach. Drop bags and freshen up after flight. Walking distance to the beach, restaurants, and metro station.

items.title: Lunch at Garota de Ipanema
items.detail: The bar where the famous bossa nova song 'Girl from Ipanema' was composed. Classic Brazilian dishes and cold chopp beer. Try the feijoada or picanha. Mains R$50–90 ($10–18).

items.title: Sugarloaf Mountain Cable Car
items.detail: Two-stage cable car to Pao de Acucar (396m). Stunning 360-degree views of Guanabara Bay, Copacabana, and Christ the Redeemer. Tickets R$120 ($24). Allow 2-3 hours. Late afternoon light is best for photos.

days.title: Rio Highlights & Flight South
days.location: Rio de Janeiro → Foz do Iguacu

items.title: Christ the Redeemer at Sunrise
items.detail: Take the cog train from Cosme Velho station up Corcovado Mountain. First train at 8 AM but early start beats the crowds. The 38-meter statue and panoramic views are breathtaking. Tickets R$80 ($16) — book online ahead.

items.title: Selaron Steps & Lapa
items.detail: The iconic 215 mosaic-covered steps created by Chilean artist Jorge Selaron. Then explore the Lapa neighborhood — Arcos da Lapa aqueduct, street art, and bohemian vibes. Free to visit.

items.title: Lunch at Confeitaria Colombo
items.detail: Belle Epoque cafe in downtown Rio since 1894. Ornate mirrors, stained glass, and classic Brazilian pastries. Try the coxinha and pasteis. Budget R$60–100. A beautiful piece of Rio history.

items.title: Flight: Rio → Foz do Iguacu
items.detail: GOL or LATAM flight from Santos Dumont (SDU) or Galeao (GIG) to Foz do Iguacu (IGU). ~2 hours. Book ahead for best fares (~R$300–500). Taxi from IGU airport to hotel ~R$40 (15 min).

items.title: Iguazu Falls — Brazilian Side
items.detail: The Brazilian side offers the best panoramic views of the 275 waterfalls spanning 2.7 km. Walk the 1.2 km trail to the Devil's Throat platform — prepare to get soaked from the spray. Entry R$80 ($16). Allow 3–4 hours.

items.title: Parque das Aves (Bird Park)
items.detail: Walk-through aviaries with toucans, macaws, and 150+ species of tropical birds. Right next to the falls entrance. Entry R$70 ($14). A great complement to the falls visit. Allow 1.5 hours.

items.title: Lunch at Rafain Churrascaria
items.detail: Legendary all-you-can-eat Brazilian churrascaria (steakhouse). Gauchos carve picanha, alcatra, and cordeiro at your table. Includes massive salad bar and sides. R$120 ($24) per person. Come hungry.

items.title: Macuco Safari Boat Ride
items.detail: Thrilling zodiac boat ride right up to the base of the falls. You will get completely drenched — bring a waterproof bag for electronics. R$280 ($56). An unforgettable experience and the highlight for most visitors.
items.weatherNote: Activity depends on river conditions — may be suspended during high water
